snapshot.stmt: For \((n,l)=(2,3)\), \(\lambda^\dagger\in\mathcal H^0_{2,3}\) is dual feasible and belongs to \(\mathcal V_{2,3}\). Its complete signature is \(\{((000),(11)),((001),(10)),((001),(11)),((010),(00)),((011),(00)),((011),(10)),((100),(01)),((100),(11)),((101),(11)),((110),(00)),((110),(01)),((111),(00))\}\). The ten type equations indexed by \(B^\dagger\) have rank \(10\), and together with \(\lambda_{(0,0,2)}=\lambda_{(0,0,3)}=0\) have rank \(12\). Moreover, for every \(v\in K_{2,3}\), \(I(\lambda+v)=I(\lambda)\); every \(\mu\in\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{2,3}\) satisfies \(I(\mu)=e_a\) for some \(a\in\{1,2,3\}\); and \(I(\lambda^\dagger)=(-1,2,1)\). Consequently \(\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{2,3}\subsetneq\mathcal V_{2,3}\) and \(|\mathcal V_{2,3}|\ge7\).

defs.def:gauge-map: For \(\lambda\in\mathcal H_{n,l}\), let \(v_z=-\lambda_{(0,0,z)}\) for \(z=2,\ldots,l\), let \(v_1=\sum_{z=2}^l\lambda_{(0,0,z)}\), set \(v_{(y,d,z)}=v_z\), and define \(\Gamma(\lambda)=\lambda+v\). Thus \(\mathcal G_{n,l}=\{\lambda:\lambda_{(0,0,z)}=0\text{ for }z=2,\ldots,l\}\) and \(\mathcal H^0_{n,l}=\mathcal H_{n,l}\cap\mathcal G_{n,l}\).
defs.def:bbak-family: For \(a\in\mathcal Z_l\) and nonconstant \(s=(s_0,\ldots,s_{n-1})\in(\mathcal Z_l\setminus\{a\})^n\), define \(w(a,s)_{y,1,j}=0\) for \(j\ne a\), \(w(a,s)_{y,1,a}=y-(n-1)\), \(w(a,s)_{y,0,j}=0\) for \(j\notin\{a,s_y\}\), \(w(a,s)_{y,0,s_y}=n-1-y\), and \(w(a,s)_{y,0,a}=-(n-1)\). The paper states this family in the lower-dual convention. In the upper-dual convention define \(\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{n,l}=\{\Gamma(\bar w(a,s)):a\in\mathcal Z_l,\ s\in(\mathcal Z_l\setminus\{a\})^n\text{ nonconstant},\  ar w(a,s)_{y,d,z}=-w(a,s)_{y,1-d,z}\}\), with \(|\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{n,l}|=l((l-1)^n-(l-1))\).
defs.def:worked-transition: Order types lexicographically by the treatment word and then by \((\eta(0),\eta(1))\). Let \(B^\dagger=\{((000),(11)),((001),(10)),((001),(11)),((010),(00)),((011),(00)),((011),(10)),((100),(01)),((100),(11)),((110),(00)),((110),(01))\}\). Replace the leaving equation \(((001),(10))\) by the entering equation \(((010),(10))\), retain the other nine equations of \(B^\dagger\), and append the gauges \(\lambda_{(0,0,2)}=\lambda_{(0,0,3)}=0\). The ten type equations have rank \(10\); with the two gauges the system has rank \(12\) and solution with blocks \((2,1,2,1)\), \((0,-1,-2,0)\), \((0,0,0,0)\), denoted \(\lambda'\). The armwise separable slack minimum is nonnegative, and its equality words give exact signature \(\{((000),(11)),((001),(11)),((010),(00)),((010),(10)),((011),(00)),((011),(10)),((100),(01)),((100),(11)),((101),(01)),((101),(11)),((110),(00)),((110),(01)),((111),(00)),((111),(01))\}\). The lexicographically least rank basis in this signature is \(B'=\{((000),(11)),((001),(11)),((010),(00)),((010),(10)),((011),(00)),((011),(10)),((100),(01)),((100),(11)),((110),(00)),((110),(01))\}\). Since \(B^\dagger\prec B'\), the proposed decreasing-basis orientation directs this edge from \(B'\) to \(B^\dagger\).

snapshot.stmt: The decreasing-canonical-basis parent rule in the reverse-search handle does not yield the claimed rooted enumeration, already for \((n,l)=(2,3)\). Its proposed root \(\rho=\Gamma(\bar w(1,(2,3)))\) has canonical basis strictly larger than the canonical basis of another vertex \(\xi\in\mathcal V_{2,3}\); hence no parent chain that strictly decreases canonical bases can lead from \(\xi\) to \(\rho\). Thus the specified construction does not establish a polynomial-delay enumerator. This obstruction does not rule out a different polynomial-delay algorithm.

defs.def:bbak-family: For \(a\in\mathcal Z_l\) and nonconstant \(s=(s_0,\ldots,s_{n-1})\in(\mathcal Z_l\setminus\{a\})^n\), define \(w(a,s)_{y,1,j}=0\) for \(j\ne a\), \(w(a,s)_{y,1,a}=y-(n-1)\), \(w(a,s)_{y,0,j}=0\) for \(j\notin\{a,s_y\}\), \(w(a,s)_{y,0,s_y}=n-1-y\), and \(w(a,s)_{y,0,a}=-(n-1)\). The paper states this family in the lower-dual convention. In the upper-dual convention define \(\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{n,l}=\{\Gamma(\bar w(a,s)):a\in\mathcal Z_l,\ s\in(\mathcal Z_l\setminus\{a\})^n\text{ nonconstant},\  ar w(a,s)_{y,d,z}=-w(a,s)_{y,1-d,z}\}\), with \(|\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{n,l}|=l((l-1)^n-(l-1))\).
defs.def:reverse-search-handle: Encode a node by the lexicographically least ordered set of \(r=2nl-l+1\) independent tight type equations on \(\mathcal G_{n,l}\); equivalently, retain those \(r\) equations together with the \(l-1\) fixed gauge equations for an ambient rank-\(2nl\) certificate. Encode \(\tau=(\delta,\eta)\) by the \(l\)-bit word \((\delta(1),\ldots,\delta(l))\) and the two labels \((\eta(0),\eta(1))\). Reconstruct the rational vertex by exact linear algebra. For one type-equation exchange, parameterize the resulting affine line; for each \((\eta(0),\eta(1))\), minimize slack over \(\delta\) armwise as \(\sum_{z=1}^l\min_{d\in\mathcal D}\lambda_{(\eta(d),d,z)}-(\eta(1)-\eta(0))\), enumerate its minimizing treatment words at equality, and thereby test feasibility, list the complete signature, and reject rank failure or unlisted tight types without expanding \(2^l n^2\) types. A candidate root is \(\Gamma(\bar w(1,(2,\ldots,2,3)))\). The required parent map chooses, among recognized one-exchange neighbors whose canonical basis is lexicographically smaller, the lexicographically first; its proof obligations are uniqueness, acyclicity, reachability to the root, and polynomially many local tests. Inputs are \(1^n,1^l\); delay counts bit operations, basis and type encodings, and rational output. The integer zero-one equation matrix, ATE right sides bounded by \(n-1\), and determinant bounds control numerator and denominator lengths.

snapshot.stmt: For every \(n\ge2\) and \(l\ge3\), \(\mathsf{VERTREC}_{n,l}(B)\) is decidable by a deterministic algorithm in bit-operation time polynomial in \(n+l+\operatorname{enc}(B)\), where \(\operatorname{enc}(B)\) is the explicit bit length of the list. The algorithm solves the equations indexed by \(B\) in the \(r=2nl-l+1\) gauge coordinates, tests the \(n^2\) separable minima \(m_{ab}(\lambda)=\sum_{z=1}^l\min\{\lambda_{(a,0,z)},\lambda_{(b,1,z)}\}\), and certifies signature completeness by comparing the listed minimizing words with the product cardinalities \(2^{t_{ab}}\), where \(t_{ab}\) is the number of tied arms.

snapshot.stmt: \(K_{n,l}=\{v:v_{(y,d,z)}=a_z\text{ for all }(y,d,z)\in\mathcal I_{n,l},\sum_{z=1}^l a_z=0\}\), so \(\dim K_{n,l}=l-1\). For every \(p\in\mathcal C_{n,l}\) and \(v\in K_{n,l}\), \(v^{\mathsf T}p=\sum_z a_z=0\). The map \(\Gamma\) is constant on each \(\sim\)-class and that class meets \(\mathcal G_{n,l}\) once. Hence \(\mathcal H^0_{n,l}\) is pointed and its vertices biject with the M-distinct vertices of \(\mathcal H_{n,l}\).

defs.def:conditional-law-polytope: \(\mathcal C_{n,l}=\{p\in\mathbb R_+^{\mathcal I_{n,l}}:\sum_{y\in\mathcal Y_n}\sum_{d\in\mathcal D}p_{y,d,z}=1\text{ for every }z\in\mathcal Z_l\}\). Consequently \(\mathbf 1^{\mathsf T}p=l\).

snapshot.stmt: The decreasing-canonical-basis parent rule in the reverse-search handle does not yield the claimed rooted enumeration, already for \((n,l)=(2,3)\). snapshot.stmt: For \(n\ge2\) and \(l\ge3\), can the parametric-separable-pivot local-neighbor construction in the reverse-search handle be coupled to an alternative globally reachable parent orientation or another explicit output-sensitive traversal to yield a deterministic rational-output algorithm \(\mathsf E_{n,l}\) that lists every element of \(\mathcal V_{n,l}\) exactly once with bit-operation delay polynomial in \(n+l\), including output encoding and termination?

defs.def:reverse-search-handle: Encode a node by the lexicographically least ordered set of \(r=2nl-l+1\) independent tight type equations on \(\mathcal G_{n,l}\); equivalently, retain those \(r\) equations together with the \(l-1\) fixed gauge equations for an ambient rank-\(2nl\) certificate. Encode \(\tau=(\delta,\eta)\) by the \(l\)-bit word \((\delta(1),\ldots,\delta(l))\) and the two labels \((\eta(0),\eta(1))\). Reconstruct the rational vertex by exact linear algebra. For one type-equation exchange, parameterize the resulting affine line; for each \((\eta(0),\eta(1))\), minimize slack over \(\delta\) armwise as \(\sum_{z=1}^l\min_{d\in\mathcal D}\lambda_{(\eta(d),d,z)}-(\eta(1)-\eta(0))\), enumerate its minimizing treatment words at equality, and thereby test feasibility, list the complete signature, and reject rank failure or unlisted tight types without expanding \(2^l n^2\) types. A candidate root is \(\Gamma(\bar w(1,(2,\ldots,2,3)))\). The required parent map chooses, among recognized one-exchange neighbors whose canonical basis is lexicographically smaller, the lexicographically first; its proof obligations are uniqueness, acyclicity, reachability to the root, and polynomially many local tests. Inputs are \(1^n,1^l\); delay counts bit operations, basis and type encodings, and rational output. The integer zero-one equation matrix, ATE right sides bounded by \(n-1\), and determinant bounds control numerator and denominator lengths.
defs.def:gauge-map: For \(\lambda\in\mathcal H_{n,l}\), let \(v_z=-\lambda_{(0,0,z)}\) for \(z=2,\ldots,l\), let \(v_1=\sum_{z=2}^l\lambda_{(0,0,z)}\), set \(v_{(y,d,z)}=v_z\), and define \(\Gamma(\lambda)=\lambda+v\). Fix \(n\ge2\), \(l\ge3\), and \(\mu\in\mathcal V_{n,l}\). Let \(\alpha_\tau\in\{0,1\}^r\) be the row of \(A_{n,l}^{\mathsf T}\) indexed by \(\tau\in\operatorname{Sig}(\mu)\), restricted to the free gauge coordinates, and define \[C_\mu=\operatorname{cone}\{\alpha_\tau:\tau\in\operatorname{Sig}(\mu)\},\qquad \mathcal A_\mu=\operatorname{conv}\{\alpha_\tau:\tau\in\operatorname{Sig}(\mu)\}.\] Then \(C_\mu\) is full-dimensional and pointed, \(\mathcal A_\mu\) is a compact affine cross-section of \(C_\mu\), and the finite extreme rays of the tangent cone at \(\mu\) are among the extreme rays of \(C_\mu^*\), which are in bijection with the facets of \(\mathcal A_\mu\). For each active outcome pair \((a,b)\), its rows form a Cartesian product of at most two choices per arm, and their convex hull is the Minkowski sum of the corresponding \(l\) singletons or segments. Consequently \(\mathcal A_\mu\) is the convex hull of at most \(n^2\) such product boxes, and exact linear optimization over \(\mathcal A_\mu\) is possible in bit-operation time polynomial in \(n+l+\operatorname{enc}(\mu)\) by armwise optimization.

u\), computes that endpoint when it exists, and decides whether \(P(

snapshot.stmt: If \(\mu\in\mathcal V_{n,l}\) satisfies \(|\operatorname{Sig}(\mu)|=r\), then all adjacent vertices \(\nu\) satisfying \(P(\nu)=\mu\) can be listed exactly once in polynomial total bit-operation time, with polynomial-time exhaustion. Let a finite rooted tree have a known root and a resumable iterator which, at each node, lists every child exactly once with delay at most \(p\) and reports exhaustion within time \(p\) after its last child. Then there is a depth-first traversal that outputs every tree node exactly once, has delay \(O(p)\), and terminates within \(O(p)\) time after its final output, irrespective of the depth of the tree.

The inverse-child relation \(\{(\mu,
u):\mu,
u\in\mathcal V_{n,l},\ P(
u)=\mu\}\) is polynomially balanced and polynomial-time decidable. Consequently, with access to an \(\mathsf{NP}\) oracle, there is a deterministic child iterator with polynomial delay and polynomial-time exhaustion, and the parity Euler traversal lists all of \(\mathcal V_{n,l}\) exactly once with global polynomial delay and polynomial-time final termination. This is an oracle upper bound and does not establish the unrelativized deterministic enumerator asked for in \(	ext{oeq:polynomial-delay-enumeration}\).

Consider the polynomial-size extended formulation of \(\mathcal K_\mu\) from \(\text{thm:explicit-global-parent-map}\). For fixed \(h\in\mathcal K_\mu\) and an active pair \((a,b)\), let \(F_{ab}(h)=\sum_z\min_{d\in R_{ab,z}(\mu)}h_{(\eta(d),d,z)}\). The fiber factor in the auxiliary variables has one vertex when \(F_{ab}(h)=0\) and \(l+1\) vertices when \(F_{ab}(h)>0\). If \(h\) is a vertex of \(\mathcal K_\mu\) and exactly \(q\) active pairs satisfy \(F_{ab}(h)>0\), then the fiber over \(h\) contains exactly \((l+1)^q\) vertices of the extended polytope, all projecting to the same normalized tangent ray.

snapshot.stmt: Let \(N_n=5\cdot4^{n-1}-2^{n+2}+4\). For every \(n\ge2\) and \(l\ge3\), \[|\mathcal V_{n,l}|\ge \binom{l}{2}(N_n-2).\] Consequently, whenever \[2(l-1)^{n-2}<N_n-2=5\cdot4^{n-1}-2^{n+2}+2,\] one has \[|\mathcal V_{n,l}|>|\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{n,l}|=l(l-1)^{n-1},\] so the literal arm-indexed family does not exhaust the gauge-fixed vertices. This criterion holds for every \(l\ge3\) when \(n=2\), for every \(n\ge2\) when \(3\le l\le5\), for \(3\le l\le25\) when \(n=3\), for \(3\le l\le12\) when \(n=4\), for \(3\le l\le9\) when \(n=5\), and for \(3\le l\le8\) when \(n=6\). These are sufficient deficit regimes; no assertion is made about the complementary pairs \((n,l)\) for which the displayed criterion fails.

defs.def:bbak-family: For \(a\in\mathcal Z_l\) and nonconstant \(s=(s_0,\ldots,s_{n-1})\in(\mathcal Z_l\setminus\{a\})^n\), define \(w(a,s)_{y,1,j}=0\) for \(j\ne a\), \(w(a,s)_{y,1,a}=y-(n-1)\), \(w(a,s)_{y,0,j}=0\) for \(j\notin\{a,s_y\}\), \(w(a,s)_{y,0,s_y}=n-1-y\), and \(w(a,s)_{y,0,a}=-(n-1)\). The paper states this family in the lower-dual convention. In the upper-dual convention define \(\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{n,l}=\{\Gamma(\bar w(a,s)):a\in\mathcal Z_l,\ s\in(\mathcal Z_l\setminus\{a\})^n\text{ nonconstant},\ \bar w(a,s)_{y,d,z}=-w(a,s)_{y,1-d,z}\}\). The indexing list has \(l((l-1)^n-(l-1))\) entries, but different indices can define the same vector because \(s_{n-1}\) multiplies a zero coefficient. As a set of distinct literal coefficient vectors, \(|\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{n,2}|=0\) and \(|\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{n,l}|=l(l-1)^{n-1}\) for \(l\ge3\).

snapshot.stmt: For every \(n\ge2\) and \(l\ge3\), the literal arm-indexed family is strictly incomplete and in fact satisfies \[|\mathcal V_{n,l}|\ge |\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{n,l}|+1=l(l-1)^{n-1}+1.\] Consequently, \( |\mathcal V_{n,l}|>|\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{n,l}|\) holds for every complementary pair satisfying \(2(l-1)^{n-2}\ge N_n-2\), as well as for every pair covered by the earlier sufficient condition.

Can one construct a deterministic rational-output child generator which, on input \(\mu\in\mathcal V_{n,l}\), lists exactly the adjacent vertices \(\nu\) satisfying \(P(\nu)=\mu\) with bit-operation delay polynomial in \(n+l\), reports exhaustion in polynomial time after its last child, and can be coupled to a traversal that lists every element of \(\mathcal V_{n,l}\) exactly once with global bit-operation delay polynomial in \(n+l\), including polynomial-time termination after the final vertex? As one sufficient route, can the relevant finite extreme rays of the tangent cone at each \(\mu\), their exact finite line-search endpoints, and the parent-filtered subset be generated with these delay and exhaustion guarantees without expanding all \(2^l n^2\) type inequalities or all active bases?

defs.def:reverse-search-handle: Encode a node by the lexicographically least ordered set of \(r=2nl-l+1\) independent tight type equations on \(\mathcal G_{n,l}\); equivalently, retain those \(r\) equations together with the \(l-1\) fixed gauge equations for an ambient rank-\(2nl\) certificate. For one type-equation exchange, parameterize the resulting affine line; for each \((\eta(0),\eta(1))\), minimize slack over \(\delta\) armwise as \(\sum_{z=1}^l\min_{d\in\mathcal D}\lambda_{(\eta(d),d,z)}-(\eta(1)-\eta(0))\), enumerate its minimizing treatment words at equality, and thereby test feasibility, list the complete signature, and reject rank failure or unlisted tight types without expanding \(2^l n^2\) types. A candidate root is \(\Gamma(\bar w(1,(2,\ldots,2,3)))\). The required parent map chooses, among recognized one-exchange neighbors whose canonical basis is lexicographically smaller, the lexicographically first; its proof obligations are uniqueness, acyclicity, reachability to the root, and polynomially many local tests. Inputs are \(1^n,1^l\); delay counts bit operations, basis and type encodings, and rational output. The integer zero-one equation matrix, ATE right sides bounded by \(n-1\), and determinant bounds control numerator and denominator lengths.
defs.def:gauge-map: For \(\lambda\in\mathcal H_{n,l}\), let \(v_z=-\lambda_{(0,0,z)}\) for \(z=2,\ldots,l\), let \(v_1=\sum_{z=2}^l\lambda_{(0,0,z)}\), set \(v_{(y,d,z)}=v_z\), and define \(\Gamma(\lambda)=\lambda+v\). snapshot.stmt: For \((n,l)=(2,3)\), there are at least \(18\) distinct vertices with exactly two essential arm blocks, six distinct vertices in the all-three-essential-arm \(S_3\)-orbit of \(\lambda^\dagger\), and at least three further distinct all-three-essential-arm permutations of the adjacent vertex \(\mu\) from the canonical-one-exchange lemma. These three collections are pairwise disjoint. Consequently \(|\mathcal V_{2,3}|\ge27\). Since the literal comparator family satisfies \(|\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{2,3}|=6\) and \(\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{2,3}\subseteq\mathcal V_{2,3}\), one has \(|\mathcal V_{2,3}\setminus\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{2,3}|\ge21\) and \(\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{2,3}\subsetneq\mathcal V_{2,3}\).

defs.def:bbak-family: For \(a\in\mathcal Z_l\) and nonconstant \(s=(s_0,\ldots,s_{n-1})\in(\mathcal Z_l\setminus\{a\})^n\), define \(w(a,s)_{y,1,j}=0\) for \(j\ne a\), \(w(a,s)_{y,1,a}=y-(n-1)\), \(w(a,s)_{y,0,j}=0\) for \(j\notin\{a,s_y\}\), \(w(a,s)_{y,0,s_y}=n-1-y\), and \(w(a,s)_{y,0,a}=-(n-1)\). The paper states this family in the lower-dual convention. In the upper-dual convention define \(\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{n,l}=\{\Gamma(\bar w(a,s)):a\in\mathcal Z_l,\ s\in(\mathcal Z_l\setminus\{a\})^n\text{ nonconstant},\ \bar w(a,s)_{y,d,z}=-w(a,s)_{y,1-d,z}\}\). The indexing list has \(l((l-1)^n-(l-1))\) entries, but different indices can define the same vector because \(s_{n-1}\) multiplies a zero coefficient. As a set of distinct literal coefficient vectors, \(|\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{n,2}|=0\) and \(|\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{n,l}|=l(l-1)^{n-1}\) for \(l\ge3\).

snapshot.stmt:
For every \(l\ge3\), at least \(6\binom l2\) vertices of \(\mathcal V_{2,l}\) have exactly two essential arm blocks and at least \(9\binom l3\) further vertices have exactly three essential arm blocks. Every member of \(\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{2,l}\) has exactly two essential arm blocks. Consequently,
\[|\mathcal V_{2,l}|\ge6\binom l2+9\binom l3=\frac32l^2(l-1),\]
and
\[|\mathcal V_{2,l}\setminus\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{2,l}|\ge\frac{l(l-1)(3l-2)}2.\]
At \(l=3\), these inequalities specialize to \(|\mathcal V_{2,3}|\ge27\) and \(|\mathcal V_{2,3}\setminus\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{2,3}|\ge21\).
